Run ID 8951.
Two samples used:
3.45 cm diameter cylinder.
Combination of 0.168-cm thick
natural lead and 0.284-cm thick 207Pb enriched to
Filters of 3.0-cm uranium and 0.3 gm/sq-cm 10B used
to reduce gamma flash and overlap of low energy
neutrons. Composite sample used to correct for 207Pb
and 208Pb in 206Pb sample. Effective purity of sample
is >99.5% 206Pb, effective thickness is
0.0989-atoms/barn. Below energy of 70 keV, no
compensation sample is used. Cross sections derived
from data using R-matrix least squares fit.
7.62-cm diameter by 2.0-cm thick NE-110
plastic scintillator with photomultiplier. Detector
placed in air in a re-entrant tube evacuated 122-cm
diameter scattering chamber.
Absolute uncertainty.
Data received from D. Wiarda, Dec. 1999.
Approved by D. Wiarda, 24 January 2000
Data added. TK.
BP: Minor corrections in SAMPLE and STATUS.
The sample consisted of a
hollow cylinder of radiogenic lead 6.35 cm long with
a 3.18-cm outer diameter and 0.254-cm wall thickness,
and was suspended at the center of the scattering
chamber by means of a fine wire.
Two runs made at three angles each, with 90-degree
angle common to both. Data normalized between both
runs using 90-degree data. Cross sections derived
using R-matrix code of Blatt and Biedenharn. Center
of mass results converted to lab frame and 'smeared'
to account for experimental resolution. Effects due
to scattering from residual air and chamber walls
were small. Multiple scattering estimates are small.
Attenuation effects were evident but not corrected for.
Neutron flux measurements used to determine relative
detector efficiency and to reduce data to relative
cross sections.
Figs. 1-8, pages 479-486.
